results.summary: This pull request (PR #43507) for the openclaw/openclaw repository introduces a fix to improve agent reliability by detecting partial tool executions before fallback retries. Specifically, it adds logic to identify when a model's fallback occurs after some tool actions have already been completed, preventing the fallback from re-executing non-idempotent operations such as API calls or commands. The update includes a new `partialExecution` field in the `FailoverError` to signal this condition, enhancing error handling and reducing unintended repeated actions. The change aims to optimize performance and robustness in agent tool execution workflows.

results.summary: This pull request introduces a significant technical update to the openclaw project by adding CLI provider support for text-to-speech (TTS). It addresses an issue where users relied solely on cloud TTS services, which could compromise privacy and increase latency. The update enables local TTS tools (like espeak, piper, mlx-audio) to be used directly within the system, enhancing privacy by keeping data on-device and supporting offline operation. The implementation includes support for template variables, environment variable passing, and improved configuration flexibility. Overall, this change improves performance, privacy, and usability by integrating local TTS capabilities into the platform.

results.summary: This pull request addresses a startup failure issue in the macOS version of openclaw. Previously, if the gateway startup failed (e.g., due to missing CLI), the onboarding wizard would still wait for a readiness timeout before displaying a generic message, leading to a poor user experience. The update modifies the `GatewayProcessManager.waitForGatewayReady()` function to now immediately return if the gateway status is `.failed`, allowing the onboarding process to fail fast and provide more accurate feedback. This fix improves error handling and responsiveness during macOS startup, enhancing overall stability and user experience.

The update involves 73 lines of code changes across five files and aims to improve the robustness of the Feishu integration under load.

# Browser Evaluate CDP Refactor Plan

## Context `act:evaluate` executes user provided JavaScript in the page. Today it runs via Playwright (`page.evaluate` or `locator.evaluate`). Playwright serializes CDP commands per page, so a stuck or long running evaluate can block the page command queue and make every later action
on that tab look "stuck". PR #13498 adds a pragmatic safety net (bounded evaluate, abort propagation, and best-effort recovery). This document describes a larger refactor that makes `act:evaluate` inherently isolated from Playwright so a stuck evaluate cannot wedge normal Playwright operations. ## Goals - Maintain backward compatibility for existing callers and payloads. - Element targeting for evaluate is supported without switching everything off Playwright. - Abort and timeout are treated the same way across HTTP and in-process dispatch. - Timeouts are single source of truth end to end so a caller can rely on a budget.  ...    ## Non-goals - Add process isolation (worker process/thread) for evaluate. If we still see hard to recover stuck states after this refactor, that is a follow-up idea.  ...    - Remove the existing safety net introduced in PR #13498 (it remains a useful fallback). - Replace all browser actions (click, type, wait, etc.) with CDP implementations. ## Current Architecture (Why It Gets Stuck)

At a high level: - A stuck queue means later click/type/wait operations on the tab can appear to hang. - Playwright serializes page commands, so an evaluate that never finishes blocks the queue. - The route handler calls into Playwright to execute the JavaScript. - Callers send `act:evaluate` to the browser control service.  ...    - Abort is propagated as an `AbortSignal` everywhere so cancellation is consistent.  ...    - Use this helper in:`src/browser/client-fetch.ts` (HTTP and in-process dispatch)`src/node-host/runner.ts` (proxy path) browser action implementations (Playwright and CDP) - Add a small helper (for example `createBudget({ timeoutMs, signal })`) that returns:`signal`: the linked  ...    - New module, for example `src/browser/cdp-evaluate.ts`, that: Connects to the configured CDP endpoint (browser level  ...    - Proposed: `{ role, name, nth, backendDOMNodeId?: number }`
- Today: `{ role, name, nth }`  ...    3. Generate the existing role ref map as today (role, name, nth).  ...    Even with CDP evaluate, there are other ways to wedge a tab or a connection. Keep the  ...    - unexpected Playwright edge cases
- environments where CDP attach is blocked

# 对于 OpenClaw 想了解一下 v 站的看法

Je236· 1 天前 · 2524 次点击 最近 OpenClaw 火得一塌糊涂，尤其在自媒体铺天盖地的安利下，很多非 IT 行业的人都开始想养一只龙虾。

个人理解 OpenClaw 和此前的豆包手机，本质上做的是同一件事——用 AI 帮用户自动完成任务。具体来说，它们通常是在设备本地（或结合云端）运行一个大语言模型，这个模型能调用各种插件；而这些插件，本质上是对各类 App 功能的封装，使得 AI 可以“操作”应用，比如发消息、查日程、下单等。 其实，这和 iOS 的快捷指令在底层逻辑上非常相似：都是依赖 APP 主动暴露的 API ，再通过某种方式（脚本、自然语言、图形化流程）组合这些能力，实现自动化。区别在于，快捷指令需要用户手动搭建流程，而 OpenClaw 这类工具试图用自然语言 + AI 推理来自动完成任务规划与执行。  ...    谈论话题

1. 消耗的 token 是否和产出成正比？
2. 龙虾真实落地的业务场景？

jingzhou· 12 小时 44 分钟前 · 339 次点击 学习用 ~2,600 行 TypeScript 从零构建一个极简版 AI 助手： Build Your Own OpenClaw欢迎 star 你是否好奇过 OpenClaw 这样的 AI 助手是怎么同时接入飞书、Telegram 等多个平台的？消息路由、Agent 工具调用循环、通道抽象又是怎么实现的？ 本教程通过构建 MyClaw（一个教学用的 OpenClaw 最小实现）来回答这些问题。12 章教程，每章聚焦一个核心模块，带你从零实现一个可运行的多通道 AI 助手。

### 你将学到什么 - 插件架构 — 控制反转（ IoC ）模式的实际应用
- Skills 系统 — 基于 Markdown 的 prompt 管理，斜杠命令调用
- WebSocket 网关 — 带认证和会话管理的实时通信服务 - 配置系统 — Zod Schema 验证 + YAML 配置的工程实践
- 消息路由 — 基于规则将不同通道的消息分发到不同 Agent/Provider - Agent 运行时 — LLM 调用、Tool Use 循环、对话历史管理的完整实现
- 通道抽象 — 如何设计统一接口，让一套代码同时服务 Terminal 、飞书、Telegram 等多个平台 ### 适合谁

- 想为自己的项目构建多通道消息网关的工程师
- 想学习 TypeScript 工程实践的中级开发者
- 想深入理解 AI 助手/Agent 架构的开发者

## 特性 - 开箱即用 —`onboard`引导配置，`doctor`健康诊断
- 插件系统 — 可扩展的运行时插件
- WebSocket 网关 — 统一 API 控制面
- 消息路由 — 规则引擎，灵活映射通道到 Agent  ...    - Coding Agent — 基于 pi-coding-agent 的内置工具集 (read/write/edit/bash)，支持多轮 Tool Use - 多 LLM — Anthropic (Claude)、OpenAI (GPT-4o)、OpenRouter (免费模型可用)
- 多通道 — Terminal + 飞书 + Telegram ，统一抽象可轻松扩展 ---

## 教程章节

本教程共 12 章，每章聚焦一个核心模块。建议按顺序阅读，每章都附有完整的代码实现和设计解析。  ...    | 01 | 项目初始化 | TypeScript + ESM 项目搭建 | `package.json`,`tsconfig.json`,`myclaw.mjs` | | 02 | CLI 框架 | Commander.js 命令系统、依赖注入 | `src/cli/program.ts`,`src/entry.ts` |  ...    | 09 | 插件系统 | 控制反转、运行时扩展 | `src/plugins/registry.ts` |  ...    3 条回复 • 2026-03-12 10:56:04 +08:00

V2EX› 分享创造 # 用 ~2,600 行 TypeScript 从零构建一个极简版 AI 助手 Build Your Own OpenClaw

jingzhou· 12 小时 44 分钟前 · 339 次点击 学习用 ~2,600 行 TypeScript 从零构建一个极简版 AI 助手： Build Your Own OpenClaw欢迎 star 你是否好奇过 OpenClaw 这样的 AI 助手是怎么同时接入飞书、Telegram 等多个平台的？消息路由、Agent 工具调用循环、通道抽象又是怎么实现的？ 本教程通过构建 MyClaw（一个教学用的 OpenClaw 最小实现）来回答这些问题。12 章教程，每章聚焦一个核心模块，带你从零实现一个可运行的多通道 AI 助手。

### 你将学到什么 - 插件架构 — 控制反转（ IoC ）模式的实际应用
- Skills 系统 — 基于 Markdown 的 prompt 管理，斜杠命令调用
- WebSocket 网关 — 带认证和会话管理的实时通信服务  ...    - Agent 运行时 — LLM 调用、Tool Use 循环、对话历史管理的完整实现
- 通道抽象 — 如何设计统一接口，让一套代码同时服务 Terminal 、飞书、Telegram 等多个平台 ### 适合谁

- 想为自己的项目构建多通道消息网关的工程师
- 想学习 TypeScript 工程实践的中级开发者
- 想深入理解 AI 助手/Agent 架构的开发者

## 特性 - 开箱即用 —`onboard`引导配置，`doctor`健康诊断
- 插件系统 — 可扩展的运行时插件
- WebSocket 网关 — 统一 API 控制面
- 消息路由 — 规则引擎，灵活映射通道到 Agent  ...    - Coding Agent — 基于 pi-coding-agent 的内置工具集 (read/write/edit/bash)，支持多轮 Tool Use - 多 LLM — Anthropic (Claude)、OpenAI (GPT-4o)、OpenRouter (免费模型可用)
- 多通道 — Terminal + 飞书 + Telegram ，统一抽象可轻松扩展 ---

## 教程章节

本教程共 12 章，每章聚焦一个核心模块。建议按顺序阅读，每章都附有完整的代码实现和设计解析。 | 章节 | 主题 | 你将学到 | 关键文件 |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | | 01 | 项目初始化 | TypeScript + ESM 项目搭建 | `package.json`,`tsconfig.json`,`myclaw.mjs` | | 02 | CLI 框架 | Commander.js 命令系统、依赖注入 | `src/cli/program.ts`,`src/entry.ts` | | 03 | 配置系统 | Zod Schema 验证、YAML 配置管理 | `src/config/schema.ts`,`src/config/loader.ts` | | 04 | 网关服务器 | WebSocket 服务、会话管理、认证 | `src/gateway/server.ts`,`src/gateway/protocol.ts` | | 05 | Agent 运行时 | LLM 抽象、Agent Loop 、InteractiveMode TUI |  ...    | 06 | 通道抽象 | 接口设计、EventEmitter 、多态 | `src/channels/transport.ts`,`src/channels/terminal.ts` |  ...    3 条回复 • 2026-03-12 10:56:04 +08:00

It explores core architectural components such as plugin architecture with inversion of control (IoC), a skills system leveraging Markdown for prompt management and slash commands, a WebSocket gateway with authentication and session management, and a configuration system utilizing Zod schema validation and YAML. The tutorial demonstrates how OpenClaw integrates multiple platforms like Feishu and Telegram through message routing, agent invocation loops, and channel abstraction. It provides practical insights into designing scalable, multi-channel AI assistants with modular architecture, real-time communication, and robust configuration management, making it valuable for developers interested in AI assistant architecture, API design, and performance optimization.
